signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for next meeting. TD Securities shifts call to preemptive 25bps hike as inflation worries persist. If RBNZ delivers, crowded spec short unwind targets 0.6000+. NZD/USD at 0.5893.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD 1W avg +0.399% vs CAD 1W avg -0.535% (spread 0.935%) — bilateral G8 divergence active. NZD/CAD supported by +75bp OIS carry advantage (RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% nominal, but NZD OIS premium persists). CAD underperforms despite positive CESI (+8) as oil volatility weighs.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3397 — 10bp SONIA-SOFR carry premium favors GBP, but 25d RR at -0.81 (put skew) signals institutional hedging against downside. GBP COT LF net long +30,708 (64.8%) is becoming crowded. VRP at +4.5% (IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.5%) indicates high protection costs ahead of US PCE data.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.40 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid; conflicting US-Iran peace plans and rebounding oil prices introduce geopolitical friction. 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal warnings. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to VIX spikes above 25.
